rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Training platform = Claude Code (CLI + Desktop) + Cowork.
Cloud/remote features carry Git dependency — out of scope.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No cloud/remote features presented as exercise steps.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Local scheduled tasks catch up on wake.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No scheduled task claims in exercise.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Verify capabilities BEFORE asserting, not after.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Platform claims checked: (1) ~/.claude/projects/ per-repo layout — verified live by ls; (2) ~/.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md auto-discover — in capability notes as verified 2026-05-14; (3) session-shaper as student-created personal skill — intentional non-shipped artifact, correctly positioned.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Compendium platform claims rot at 60 days.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No compendium platform claims being authored; judge reading only, not writing compendium entries.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Maintain living capability notes; consult before asserting.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Consulted reference_claude_code_capabilities.md before evaluating all platform claims. Cross-project ~/.claude/projects/ read pattern is established in curriculum (ae101-m4-locate-transcript.md) and verified by live ls showing per-project folders.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't extend known mechanism into adjacent context without verifying.
rules_evaluated.evidence: study prompt extends single-project transcript reading to cross-project scan. Pattern verified live (ls ~/.claude/projects/ shows per-repo folders with .jsonl files). No syntax extension into an untested runtime.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Fix the contract, not the broken recovery.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No broken-recovery patterns found in exercise prompts.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Practitioner observation beats docs lookup.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No conflicting practitioner observation vs. docs situations in this audit.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Live-test beats docs for runtime-behavior claims.
rules_evaluated.evidence: ~/.claude/projects/ layout verified live. ~/.claude/skills/test-strategy/ confirmed present (ls ~/.claude/skills/). session-shaper not pre-existing (student creates it) — correctly not checked for existence.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Known correct Claude Code facts.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Exercise body: 'It lives at ~/.claude/skills/session-shaper/SKILL.md and auto-discovers in every future session you run, across every repo.' Matches capability notes: personal skills at ~/.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md auto-discover for all projects on this machine. CLAUDE.local.md as personal gitignored config correctly named. M6 opens new session in M5 worktree — per §7b cross-cwd boundary requires new, not resume.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: When tool feature behaves differently than expected, update curriculum.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No known behavior mismatch between curriculum claims and actual behavior in this file.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Auto-load surface = four things.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Exercise correctly names CLAUDE.local.md as personal layer (gitignored). No incorrect auto-load claims. ~/.claude/skills/ referenced as personal skill home (not auto-load in the CLAUDE.md sense).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Compendium pointers inside files = autoload-redundant.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Exercise is student-facing content, not a memory/compendium/SKILL.md file.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Transcript-by-mtime selection fragile when current session writes target folder.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Exercise body and spot-gaps-1 prompt explicitly instruct reading from COORDINATES stored in task.md and plan.md (protected Run coordinates block), not by mtime scan. Satisfies marker-based selection requirement.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: PreToolUse/PostToolUse hooks read env from harness, not from agent Bash subshells.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Exercise does not discuss hook behavior or env propagation.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Sessions = unit of work — write 'start a new session in X.'
rules_evaluated.evidence: Exercise header widget: 'Session *(new, "Module 6 worktree session")*'. Body: 'Open a new Claude Code session in the existing M5 worktree.' Correct verb and framing.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cross-working-directory boundaries always open new; evidence travels by disk.
rules_evaluated.evidence: M6 opens a NEW session in the M5 worktree (different cwd from original repo). Both run artefacts travel by disk via task.md/plan.md coordinates, git refs, and transcript paths written to coordinates — not by session resume across cwd.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Same-cwd recovery defaults to --resume, not new.
rules_evaluated.evidence: M6 crosses cwd boundary (new worktree session), so §7b applies. No same-cwd recovery callout needed.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: CLAUDE.md describes runtime, not just task.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No non-standard runtime (cloud, container, network share) in this exercise.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: F-Secure curriculum off-limits.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No F-Secure references.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No premature infrastructure.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No infrastructure proposals in exercise.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: External org content — IP boundary.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No external org content imported.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Destructive operations need confirmation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No destructive file operations (rule-cutting in CLAUDE.local.md is surgical edit, not bulk delete).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No as-is imports from internet.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No internet imports.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Pseudo-URI + kill-switch for public/private content split.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No private content references in this exercise.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Internal audit/session-retro directories default-gitignore from creation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Exercise does not create audit directories.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Quality gates run at wind-down, not mid-session.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Process rule, not applicable to exercise content.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Tooling test layers carry acceptance-test contract.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Exercise is student-facing content, not a tooling test harness.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Script ratchet — LLM orchestration surfaces ratchet toward script-replaceable assertions.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Tooling process rule, not applicable to exercise content.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Hook lints partition into auto-fix vs warn.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Hook configuration rule, not applicable to exercise content.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Runner ↔ source pre-flight.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Runner tooling rule, not applicable to exercise content.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Judge must honor script exit codes.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Tooling process rule.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Eval-fire writing-class fires must schema-validate before presenting verdicts.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Eval orchestration rule, not applicable to exercise content.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Coverage tools enumerate universe, not index of past work.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Tooling design rule.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cowork sandboxed to its connected working directories.
rules_evaluated.evidence: AE101 is Code-only (no Cowork). No Cowork runtime assumptions in exercise.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: maintainer_acceptance = JSON-level override for accepted/mitigated judge verdicts.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Eval process rule.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cowork capability surface — UI surfaces subagent reads.
rules_evaluated.evidence: AE101 is Code-only.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Audit scope derives from TRAININGS registry + link-walk.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Audit scoping rule for orchestrator, not applicable to exercise content.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Acceptance-test contract covers prompt mechanics + end-state artefacts, NOT shell execution.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Mechanical battery rule.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-doc drift surfaces via judge citation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Strategy-doc drift process rule.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Portability audits: analyze agent's runtime, not prompt's literal syntax.
rules_evaluated.evidence: All shell syntax in prompts (git refs, file paths) is agent-run, not student-typed. Per §29 this is not a portability risk.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Skills at ~/.claude/skills/<name>/ are user-global but per-codebase in content — namespace by SUT.
rules_evaluated.evidence: session-shaper is intentionally generic at the name level but codebase-specific in content — body: 'The shape follows what the two runs demanded, not a template.' Personal first (ships to ~/.claude/skills/session-shaper/) and team-promoted via conversation.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: state.json files are convenience caches, not authoritative.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Exercise reads from canonical SUT-side artefacts (task.md, plan.md coordinates) not from state.json. Correct per §31.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: tmux-runner sessions must use per-runner socket.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Tooling infrastructure rule.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Source URL + type label — mandatory; maintainer-block scope.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Klaassen cite at exercise file L125 carries URL https://every.to/source-code/compound-engineering-the-definitive-guide and label [practitioner direct, vendor venue]. URL confirmed alive (HTTP/2 200, checked 2026-05-31). Ramp Dojo and Intercom Tier 1/2/3 entries in Frameworks are 'convergent practitioner vocabulary' phrasing without specific numbers — no URL required for that framing.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: OODA paraphrase ≠ primary source.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No OODA paraphrase pipeline used for exercise body claims.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Freshness rule — 6 months.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Klaassen every.to article (compound-engineering-the-definitive-guide) — publication Aug 2025, within 6 months of 2026-05-31. No other time-sensitive citations in exercise body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Zombie stat guard.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No round-number AI failure rates in exercise body or prompt content. Three skill shapes (sharpened verifier / LLM-judge / gap-finder) are typological framing, not statistics.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Hold short OODA loose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Exercise does not present OODA findings as conclusions.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Resist prescriptive post-OODA phrasings.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No prescriptive post-OODA phrasings in exercise.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Default to asking before folding findings into strategy docs.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No strategy doc updates in exercise.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Vision × Detail split.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Exercise body claims ('the session-shaper takes one of three forms, from the convergence of practitioners running long tasks') are practitioner-convergent framing without attaching specific numbers. Vision layer clean.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Grep continuous-research/ and memory/ before guessing at a name.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Process rule for generation; not applicable to exercise audit.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Consult research BEFORE generating, not after.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Process rule for generation.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No 'What research says' callout boxes.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No research callout boxes in exercise body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Source-verification maintainer block required on student-facing files with practitioner citations + specific numbers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Exercise body carries no specific practitioner names or load-bearing numbers. Klaassen is named only in maintainer block Frameworks section (below <!-- maintainer -->) which is audit/provenance vocabulary, not student-facing body. Full source verification with freshness stamps resides in the training module file. §11 fires on 'named practitioner + specific URL + load-bearing number' in student-facing body — all three thresholds clear.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No placeholders inside fenced blocks.
rules_evaluated.evidence: All prompt bodies scanned in expanded output (8 prompts: spot-gaps-1, -2, -study, -shapes, -primitives, -3, -4, -5). No [BRACKETS] or named placeholder slots found. Prompts reference session artifacts via back-reference language or named paths.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block: one-sentence action lead-in with command verb, <20 words.
rules_evaluated.evidence: All three Phase-2 lead-ins now satisfy rule 2 as standalone one-sentence command-verb lead-ins immediately before their markers. study (L49): standalone 'Ask Claude to scan your sessions across every project and group the kinds of work that recur.' — command verb, 18 words, immediately before marker. shapes (L55): standalone 'Ask Claude to draw your top few work-shapes as simple diagrams.' — command verb, <20 words, immediately before marker. primitives (L63): standalone 'Ask Claude to name the checking primitives the field already runs and rank the ones that fit your gap.' — command verb, <20 words, now isolated as its own paragraph immediately before {{prompt:spot-gaps-build-the-loop-primitives}} at L65. Prior REVISE resolved.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Installed Skills invoke by name, never path.
rules_evaluated.evidence: spot-gaps-build-the-loop-3 prompt body references '~/.claude/skills/test-strategy/SKILL.md' as a path inside the fenced block. Known deferred per session context — gated prompt edit requiring maintainer-approval pass.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Replace path reference with name-based language: 'Same authoring approach as the `test-strategy` skill.' Claude auto-discovers; path is noise. Requires maintainer-approval card per check_prompts.md §22.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Chain by back-reference + deterministic path, never invented alias.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Chains verified: shapes prompt opens 'Take the recurring groups you just named' (back-ref to study output in scrollback). primitives prompt references 'the dominant gap I named in Phase 1' (back-ref to spot-gaps-1 output). spot-gaps-3 references authoring pattern from test-strategy skill. spot-gaps-5 invokes session-shaper by name (from spot-gaps-3 write to ~/.claude/skills/session-shaper/SKILL.md). All requires/produces frontmatter correctly maps the chain.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Prompts live in registry; module/exercise/lecture files reference by marker.
rules_evaluated.evidence: All 8 prompt markers resolve correctly: spot-gaps-build-the-loop-1 through -5, plus -study, -shapes, -primitives all have corresponding files in curriculum/prompts/. Expanded output confirms clean resolution.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body (prompt-encodes-the-mechanism angle).
rules_evaluated.evidence: Key forcing functions are inside prompt fences: spot-gaps-1 encodes 'For every claim, quote a specific moment from the artefacts. Don't generalise.' spot-gaps-3 encodes 'Push back when my answer is generic.' spot-gaps-4 encodes 'Don't reassure me. Name weak parts.' Body commentary explains WHY these matter (push-back moves), but ENFORCEMENT is in the prompt text.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Rule that does not force is worse than no rule.
rules_evaluated.evidence: All rules in exercise prompt fences are mechanically enforceable: 'quote a specific moment', 'one question at a time', 'find the one rule', 'Don't reassure me'. No unenforceable rules found.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Skeptical pushbacks are re-verify triggers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Exercise body contains multiple explicit re-verify triggers: 'If Claude writes ...without naming which commit... re-run the prompt with the quote rule re-asserted.' 'If the diff touches more than the one rule you flagged, push back.' 'If Claude narrates a plan... ask it to skip the preamble.' All pushbacks lead to concrete re-verify actions.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Verification machinery needs a smoke-test.
rules_evaluated.evidence: spot-gaps-5 invokes the session-shaper skill on the M5 packaged re-run as the smoke-test: 'Now invoke the skill on the packaged run you just diffed. Authoring without invocation is theatre.' The invocation IS the verification smoke-test for the authored skill.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Context-loading tiers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Exercise correctly stages context loading: Phase 1 reads from disk coordinates (task.md, plan.md), Phase 2 study reads ~/.claude/projects/ cross-project, Phase 2 author reads test-strategy skill as template reference. Each phase adds context as needed rather than dumping all context at start.

platform_claims_checked.claim: Phase 1 reads from coordinates stored in task.md and plan.md, not from branch or transcript search

platform_claims_checked.claim: Mermaid diagrams drawn in shapes prompt (draw each one as a mermaid diagram)

Three skill shapes — convergent practitioner vocabulary across Ramp Dojo, Intercom Tier 1/2/3 (no specific numbers claimed, framed as convergent vocabulary)
